objectives: Grade the MT940 Block 2 Output Header fix in camt053_to_mt940.rs
Verify correctness of the format string per SWIFT spec
Verify test adequacy after workaround removal
constraints: Block 2 Output must be exactly 47 chars: O(1)+msg_type(3)+input_time(4)+MIR(28)+output_date(6)+output_time(4)+priority(1)
important_facts: The new format string produces 49 chars instead of the required 47 - has 2 extra zeros after the sender_lt in the post-MIR section
The parser still accepts the output because it only requires >= 45 chars after O and slices fixed positions, but priority at [45] reads '0' instead of 'N'
Priority '0' doesn't match S|N|U filter so becomes None instead of Some('N')
The roundtrip test passes because parse_mt940 only validates block4 fields, not block2 structure
pad_lt_address is duplicated between camt053_to_mt940.rs and helpers.rs
decisions_made: FAILED: format string has 2 extra zero characters, producing non-spec-compliant Block 2 output
risks_issues: Block 2 non-compliance: any downstream system parsing the generated MT940 would get wrong priority or fail stricter validation
No test validates block 2 structure of generated output

iteration.summary: Graded the MT940 Block 2 fix. The fix improved the old format (39 chars, too short to parse) to a new format that parses, but introduced 2 extra zero characters making the output 49 chars instead of the spec-required 47. The priority field is silently lost. Tests pass only because they don't validate block 2 correctness. Score: 6/10 FAILED.
next_actions: Fix format string to remove 2 extra zeros
Add test that validates block 2 length and priority of generated output
Consider extracting pad_lt_address to shared module
